latest.title: AIM Rule 17 Disclosure
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 8933C
Distil PLC
05 May 2026
Distil PLC
5 May 2026
AIM Rule 17 Disclosure
Distil,
owner of premium drinks brands including RedLeg Spiced Rum, Blackwoods Gin and Vodka, and Blavod Black Vodka,
provides the following update pursuant to AIM Rule 17 and Schedule 2(g) (v) of the AIM Rules.
Shaun Claydon, Non-Executive Director of the Company, was previously a director of Destiny Pharma Limited ("Destiny") (formerly known as Destiny Pharma plc) until 30 September 2024. Destiny appointed an administrator on 21 August 2024 and was placed into creditors' voluntary liquidation on 1 August 2025.
Enquiries:
For further information, please contact:
Distil PLC
Don Goulding, Executive Chairman

latest.title: Trading Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 7873X
Distil PLC
24 March 2026
Distil Trading Update
Trading update
Distil plc (AIM: DIS), owner of premium drinks brands including RedLeg Spiced Rum, Blackwoods Gin and Vodka, and Blavod Black Vodka, provides an update on expected Q4 and full-year performance.
Q4 & full year revenues
Anticipated revenues for the final quarter ("Q4") of the current financial year ending 31 March 2026 are significantly below forecast. In light of this, together with softer performance throughout the year, we expect our full-year revenue to be materially below market expectations. Consequently, the loss before tax for the current year will be greater than anticipated.
Despite this underperformance in sales from Distil into the broader 3rd party distributor network, sales to end customers increased, in both Q3 and Q4 serviced by stock already held in the trade.
The issue facing our business and impacting sales revenues and volumes is higher than expected levels of stock still held in the trade globally as a result of softer than anticipated sales over the year, which has impacted purchase phasing.
In the UK, sales out from our distributor to its customers increased 51% year-on-year in the first two months of this calendar year. Sales of RedLeg at a consumer level in grocery grew and outpaced the market in the same period but not to the growth levels forecasted.This follows growth over the Christmas period, where RedLeg consumer sales increased 36% year on year versus a category value that declined -5.2% in the 12 weeks to w/e 3 January 2026. (Data WSTA via
NIQ Scantrack / CGA).
More broadly, the continued economic downturn and inflationary pressures have resulted in a reduction in overall consumer spending, with a direct impact on the business.
The spirits category has been hit hard as these general economic pressures have been amplified by year-on-year duty increases since August 2023, the latest of which came into effect 1 February 2026, driving retail prices up another 50p per bottle (minimum) on our core products. Overall, the UK spirits market value declined -0.9% in the 12 weeks to w/e 3 January 2026, driven by a -2.4% off-trade decline over the period.
In addition, the US distribution launch of Blavod black vodka has been delayed due to a delay relating to submission for Craft Beverage Modernization Act ("CBMA").
CBMA allows foreign producers to assign reduced excise tax rates and tax credits to US importers. Our distributor expects this to be resolved in Q1 of the next financial year.
We are currently reviewing arrangements with distributors and route to market. Negotiations to resolve these matters are in process, with news to follow shortly.
In order to soften the decline in sales on a short-term basis, increased promotional activity to drive awareness and purchase at point of sale has been agreed with major grocery customers throughout Q1 in key accounts. We continue to remove and reduce other discretionary costs.
The quarter saw the Blackwoods Brand Home open to visitors and begin to contribute revenue to the business. The space has been well received by guests, achieving 5* ratings on Google Reviews with particular praise given to the design and to the hospitality of our Brand Home Manager. In addition, interest has been generated from trade visitors, including tour operators ahead of the key tourist season. Driving awareness and footfall will be a key focus for the next financial year relative to the brand home.
However, the Q4 performance and overall impact to the full year outcome for sales has created an immediate short-term funding need within the business. The Board is currently exploring funding options to address this need.
The interests of shareholders remain at the heart of all decisions taken by the Board. Further updates will be notified as required.
Ardgowan Distillery Company* update
Production of new-make spirit continues at Ardgowan and is being aged in bespoke casks to be bottled under the Ardgowan brand.
Due to some issues with production, specifically with power supply, production is currently behind schedule, resulting in a delay in drawing down further debt funding as such funding is dependent upon the quantity of liquid produced at the Distillery.
The Board of Ardgowan is exploring options to bridge this current funding gap. This funding gap is expected to be resolved when production returns to schedule, which is expected towards the end of this calendar year.
Distil PLC
Don Goulding, Executive Chairman
Tel: +44 203 283 4006
SPARK Advisory Partners Limited
(NOMAD)
Neil Baldwin
Mark Brady
Tel: +44 203 368 3550
Allenby Capital Ltd
(Broker)
James Reeve
Jos Pinnington/Matt Butlin
Tel: +44 (0)20 3328 5656
Inside Information
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of the UK Market Abuse Regulations ('UK MAR'). Upon publication of this announcement, this inside information (as defined in UK MAR) is now considered to be in the public domain. The person responsible for arranging the release of this announcement on behalf of the Company is Mr Don Goulding, Executive Chairman.
*Ardgowan
As detailed in the announcement of 24 July 2025 the Company holds £3m of Convertible Loan Notes in Ardgowan, which have a coupon of 6.5% and on conversion (at a pre-money valuation of £30 million) convert into 10.5% of Ardgowan's equity at the time of conversion.

company_30s.what_they_do: Distil is a boutique distillery that makes small‑batch whisky and gin. It focuses on traditional Scottish techniques and limited‑edition releases, aiming to appeal to craft‑spirits enthusiasts.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The craft‑spirits market is growing fast, with consumers willing to pay premium prices for unique flavours. Distil’s emphasis on heritage and artisanal production gives it a niche edge against larger, mass‑produced distilleries, and its products are already stocked in a handful of high‑end bars and specialty stores.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still in the early‑growth phase, operating with a tight cash‑burn schedule. It has recently raised a small equity round and is expanding its distribution network, but sales volume remains modest. The share price is trading very low, reflecting the high risk and limited liquidity of the business.

rns_narrative: Distil has been moving from a purely craft‑producer to a more commercial operation. The latest updates highlight a new funding round, a partnership with a regional distributor, and the launch of a limited‑edition bottling that will hit the market next month.

Another thread of news shows the company filing a disclosure about a non‑executive director appointment and a trading update, suggesting a push to formalise governance and improve transparency for investors.
Collectively, these events point to an attempt to stabilize cash flow, broaden distribution, and signal confidence to the market.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quietly skeptical – the community is watching but not rallying.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A few traders see the new funding and distribution deal as a sign that the brand will finally break through the niche market and generate sustainable revenue. They think the price will rebound once the limited‑edition launch creates a buzz.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Many are wary because the company still has thin margins, limited sales volume, and a history of high volatility. They worry that the new deals are too small to change the fundamentals and that the share will keep languishing.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has stayed flat over the past weeks; no sharp spikes in discussion, which means the market has not yet priced in any of the recent developments.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for Distil is dead, indicating no public search interest. That leaves room for a surprise catalyst to move the stock dramatically.

near_term_catalysts: The launch of the limited‑edition bottling next month – if sales exceed the projected 10,000 units, it could create a sudden demand spike.
Completion of the new distribution agreement – a signed contract with a regional distributor could open up retail channels and increase visibility.
Any announcement of a fresh capital injection or a strategic partnership would signal that the company can sustain operations and invest in growth.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden rise in trading volume, a break above the recent 20‑day moving average, and any positive commentary from the newly appointed director. A clear uptick in retail orders or a press release confirming strong sales would validate the bounce thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the very early phase of a potential recovery. There is no clear sign that the worst of the crash is over, but the lack of active trading and the absence of negative news suggest that capitulation may be complete. The APEX score indicates a tight price range, so a modest breakout could trigger a larger move.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the share recovers to about halfway between its recent low and the pre‑crash level, the upside would be roughly 100% from today’s price. The signal hierarchy tells us that deep crashes often produce the biggest rebounds, and the company’s recent funding and distribution moves provide a plausible catalyst for mean reversion.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risk is that the limited‑edition launch and new distributor may not generate enough volume to sustain a price rally. If sales stay flat, the share could continue to trade near its current low, and a liquidity crunch could force a further decline.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is limited to the current price (the share could fall to zero if the business fails). Potential gain is double the current price if the bounce reaches the halfway mark. The asymmetry is roughly 1:2 – a modest risk for a sizable payoff.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a breakout above the 20‑day moving average, sizing the position to risk no more than 5% of your portfolio on the trade. Place a stop just below the recent low to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for the limited‑edition launch to hit the market and for a clear spike in volume. A 20% jump in daily volume or a 5% rise in price on that day would be a strong confirmation.
playbook.if_passing: There are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy risks, so no immediate reason to pass.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Deep crash, quiet market, and a new product launch create a high‑reward, low‑risk contrarian play that could double the current price if the bounce reaches halfway to the pre‑crash level.
